text.title: Two-Layer Secret Scanning Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the two-layer secret-scan discipline and skip path.

rule.statement: Every repo MUST run gitleaks as a pre-commit hook on the developer or agent machine — sub-second, blocks commit before secret enters local git history.
rule.rationale: Pre-commit is the only layer that prevents the secret from ever existing in local git history.

rule.statement: CI MUST run TruffleHog with `--results=verified` so candidate tokens are validated against the issuing provider (AWS STS, GitHub, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic). Unverified findings are advisory only.
rule.rationale: Provider verification eliminates false-positive noise and isolates real live tokens.

rule.statement: A verified-live finding MUST trigger immediate rotation + revert. Issue automation MUST page the security owner; no merge until rotation confirmed.
rule.rationale: Live tokens are exploitable in minutes; rotation must precede or accompany revert.

rule.statement: `gitleaks.toml` MUST carry an explicit allowlist of test fixtures + documentation examples; entries with no comment explaining "why allowed" are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unexplained allowlist entries become persistent bypass routes.

rule.statement: Migrations of historical secrets MUST pair with `detect-secrets` baseline so legacy findings can be ratcheted out without blocking every PR.
rule.rationale: Without baseline, legacy findings flood the gate and adoption stalls.

rule.statement: CI's gitleaks job MUST use the same `gitleaks.toml` as the pre-commit hook so allowlists do not drift between layers.
rule.rationale: Different allowlists between layers create silent gaps.

rule.statement: If CI has no outbound egress AND no offline provider-format substitute is acceptable, OR repo is a read-only vendor mirror, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when verification cannot be performed and the team accepts the gap.
